content.problemStatement:
The country of Cheflandia has NNN cities, with MMM **one-directional** roads connecting pairs of them.

There are also flights between certain pairs of cities. In particular, there's a flight from city uuu to city vvv if and only if:


There exists a way to travel from city uuu to city vvv via some sequence of roads; **and**
There exists a way to travel from city vvv to city uuu via some sequence of roads.
Chef is planning to go on a tour of Cheflandia. A tour plan is a sequence of k≥1k\geq 1k≥1 cities

(v1,v2,…,vk)(v_1, v_2, \ldots, v_k)(v1​,v2​,…,vk​) such that:


All the viv_ivi​ are distinct, i.e, each city is visited at most once.
For each 1≤i<k1 \leq i \lt k1≤i<k, either there exists a road directly from viv_ivi​ to vi+1v_{i+1}vi+1​, or it is possible to fly from viv_ivi​ to vi+1v_{i+1}vi+1​.
Note that the tour is allowed to start and end anywhere, and can be of any length kkk (as long as the rules above are followed).

Two tour plans are considered different if:


A city is visited in one of them but not in the other; or
The order in which cities are visited is different.
constraints: Each ordered pair (u,v)(u, v)(u,v) appears at most once in the input.
The sum of NNN and the sum of MMM across all tests both won't exceed 3⋅1053\cdot 10^53⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Find the number of distinct tour plans Chef can come up with.
This number can be large, so print it modulo 998 244 353998\ 244\ 353998 244 353.
Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM — the number of cities and the number of roads.
The next MMM lines describe the roads. The iii-th of these MMM lines contains two space-separated integers uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​, denoting a one-directional road from city uiu_iui​ to city viv_ivi​.
Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of distinct tour plans, modulo 998 244 353998\ 244\ 353998 244 353.

Explanation:
Test case 111: There are five tour plans, namely:
(1),(2),(3),(2,3),(3,2)(1), (2), (3), (2, 3), (3, 2)(1),(2),(3),(2,3),(3,2).
Note that it's allowed to start and end at the same city as long as no other cities are visited.
Traveling from city 222 to 333 (or vice versa) can be done either via road or via flight; but we don't differentiate between them.
Test case 333: There are 666 cities and no roads connecting any of them, so the only possible tour plans are to start and end at the same city, giving us 666 possibilities.
